ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Roti John; mapped to closest parent class 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_03315595) as Roti John is an open-faced sandwich-style dish. Exact match unavailable.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Sandwich) used as closest defensible category. No dedicated Roti John code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Roti John or equivalent Singaporean street food sandwich. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Roti John is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one half of a standard French baguette filled with chicken-egg mixture and melted cheese.

nutrient_source.title: Roti John Chicken with Cheese — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Roti John Chicken with Cheese (cr_id G00010884). All per-100g values are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — white bread / baguette-based sandwich analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Roti John Chicken with Cheese. GI estimated at 72 based on the dominant carbohydrate carrier being a white French baguette, which has a published GI of approximately 70–75 in the University of Sydney GI Database (white bread analogous entry). The overall dish GI is moderated slightly by protein and fat content but remains in the high category. GL per serving calculated as: GI (72) × available carbohydrate per serving (13.3 g for 150 g serving) / 100 = 9.6, rounded to 9.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1279 mg per 100 g — more than double the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Roti John Chicken with Cheese; a single 150 g serving delivers approximately 1919 mg sodium, nearly the entire recommended daily limit of 2000 mg, primarily from processed cheese, condiments, and seasoning.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white baguette base (GI ~72) offset partially by high protein (16.1 g/100 g) and moderate fat slowing glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Roti John Chicken with Cheese in small portions; the white baguette raises postprandial blood glucose rapidly, and the sweet chilli sauce and sugar content (6.2 g/100 g) add further glycaemic load — substituting a wholemeal roll and omitting sweet sauces is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~6.5 g/100 g from cheese and butter) combined with very high sodium (1279 mg/100 g) and cholesterol (78 mg/100 g) presents a compounded cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Roti John Chicken with Cheese; the combination of saturated fat, high sodium, and dietary cholesterol elevates LDL cholesterol and blood pressure simultaneously, and a 150 g serving contributes approximately 34.6 g total fat and 117 mg cholesterol.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.5 g/100 g) exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; pan-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); sodium at 1279 mg/100 g worsens inflammatory tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in its standard preparation; reducing cheese quantity, replacing butter-frying with grilling, and requesting reduced-salt preparation are the minimum modifications needed to lower pro-inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roti John Chicken with Cheese is a popular Singaporean street food and hawker dish consisting of a split French baguette (roti) pan-fried with a seasoned minced chicken and egg mixture pressed onto the cut surface, then topped with melted processed cheese and served with chilli sauce and mayonnaise. The name 'Roti John' is believed to derive from Malay street vendors addressing British colonial-era customers as 'John', with 'roti' meaning bread in Malay. The dish has Indian-Malay culinary roots and is widely sold at hawker centres, food courts, and pasar malam (night markets) across Singapore and Malaysia. Texturally, the exterior of the bread is crisp and golden from pan-frying in margarine or butter, while the interior remains soft; the egg-chicken layer is savoury and slightly caramelised, and the melted cheese adds a rich, creamy, salty note. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 307 kcal, 16.1 g protein, 23.1 g fat, and 8.9 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is very high at 1279 mg/100 g, driven by processed cheese, condiments, and seasoning, making it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Saturated fat at approximately 6.5 g/100 g from cheese and butter raises LDL cholesterol concerns. The white baguette base carries a high glycaemic index (~72), relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. Calcium is notably high at 1375 mg/100 g due to the processed cheese content. The dish is typically halal-certified when prepared with chicken and halal cheese.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010884) at confidence 0.95. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the white baguette as the dominant carbohydrate carrier, which is clearly documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin profile, and moisture data for this specific Roti John preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010884) as of 2026. saturated_fat_g (6.5 g/100 g) is estimated from the known composition of processed cheese (~20 g saturated fat per 100 g cheese) and butter/margarine contribution at typical recipe proportions, consistent with the HPB total fat value of 23.06 g/100 g; this is an internal derivation and not directly reported by HPB. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source, consistent with white baguette and processed cheese composition containing negligible fibre. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white French baguette (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for Roti John Chicken with Cheese exists in any approved source. Halal status assessed as likely true given chicken and standard hawker preparation, but individual vendor certification should be verified. Cultural origin is classified as Singaporean (Indian-Malay fusion) rather than purely Indian despite the input record listing primary_cuisine as Indian; Roti John is a distinctly Singaporean street food with Malay-Indian roots.
